entry.title: Show result of custom action in popup window
entry.content:
Hello, experts

Didn't find solution for subject.

My question is is it possible to create custom action in behavior of entity which collects the data and show it in popup window?

My task is collect data for keys which entity contains and show that data in popup screen.

Thank you for any suggestion in advance.

entry.title: Re: How to create custom OData service in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content:
@ranjeetkumar07

Hello,

I am facing same issue, not able to see the OData service in maintain form templates App in public cloud. 

Waiting for your valuable input.

 

@ranjeetkumar07

entry.title: SD Pricelist (TA: V_NLN) - Layout
entry.content:
Hello,

in the SD pricelist report (TA: V_NLN) there are two badis which are called:

- BADI_PIQ_SDPRICELIST

- BADI_PIQ_SDPRICELIST_OUTPUT

with a couple of methods to change the processing/result of the report.

1.) Is there a way to adjust the ALV layout, e.g. have the option for 'Optimized Width' or 'Zebra' pattern active (w/o modification)?

2.) Is there a way to tell the system to download an excel file with extension of .xlsx instead of the old extension .xls

Thanks for you answers and ideas,

bapimueller/Thomas

entry.title: Re: Enabling SAP Build Process Automation's My Inbox App in SAP Build Work Zone, Standard Edition
entry.content: @sapbtpuser perhaps re-check again the destination configuration, just to make sure the `sap_process_automation_service` destination fields & values configuration matched from the service key that you have generated SAP Build Process Automation service instance.

entry.title: Re: Qual formato devemos seguir para o próximo encontro SAP Meet up? Compartilhe suas Preferências
entry.content:
@Marssel700 , primeiro...parabens pela ideia de ouvir a comunidade sobre este tema!

Eu gosto e acho util os Workshops, Webinares e Eventos de networking. Acho interessante o format dos SIT pois me parece que conseguem oferecer um pouco de todos estes aspectos, dependendo do formato de organização!

entry.title: Call for speakers - SAP Stammtisch Plus Hyderabad :- 3rd Aug 2024
entry.content:
Hello SAP Community!

We are gearing up to host an in-person SAP Stammtisch Plus event 

Date, Time & Location

3rd Aug 2024 (SATURDAY)
From 9:00 AM  onwards
SAP Callidus Cloud, 6th floor, Building No. 12-B, Raheja Mindspace IT Park (Google Maps)

 With the success of last SIT Hyderabad events and the excitement of community folks, 

If you are interested to be part of this Event as Speaker register Call for Speakers Stammtisch + 

Note: This is an In-person Event only

If you have any questions, please get in touch with us at sapcommunityhyderabad@gmail.com. We look forward to reviewing your session submission and featuring your expertise at the event.

Please help spread the word about speaker registration by sharing this post with your network.

 

Thanks & Regards
Ruthvik Chowdary

entry.title: Re: eInvoice Email-Process - Process Status is stuck on "...
entry.content:
Dear @Uwe_K,
i have changed the settings but if i check the connection of the destination (with the certificate i created with your guide) i get the following message: "Connection to "0M5PLFE" established. Response returned: "401: Unauthorized",  thats why i tried to use the other certificate. I have also tried to reprocess the documents in the peppol exchange app but the "reprocess button" is grayed out and i cant click it. Does this mean i have to delete and recreate the participant ?

entry.title: Re: Highlight fields in EC
entry.content: Hi @alexandermenz, it doesn't have to be required, we would like to have only some colour highlight for this field when we are doing changes to notice that value in this field will be changed (due to the business rule which is configured).

entry.title: Re: Hello SAP World
entry.content:
Great @Sebastian_hok.

Welcome to SAP Community

entry.title: Re: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST Error G/L account is not defin...
entry.content: GL_ACCOUNT already has the leading zeroes so FM is not needed. When I debug the BAPI in program, there was already leading zeroes in GL_ACCOUNT like '0006000170'. Above code is also not hardcoded, I just modified for easy reading.

entry.title: Re: Welcome to the beginner corner!
entry.content:
Hi, @David_Chaviano.

As a woman, I want to participate in the digital skills initiative to become SAP certified. I also want to learn from other people's perspectives and use them in my daily work as a Finance Specialist.

Thank you!

entry.title: Re: How to create custom OData service in S/4HANA Cloud?
entry.content: @AnilKumar12 - i will update you soon, just give me sometime.

entry.title: Re: Boost your ADT efficiency with these new updates for the DevEpos Add-On Family
entry.content:
Thanks for those great addons!

For any reason when opening the search tool, I see an error an the bottom of window in tab Abap Code Search (DevEpos) and a similar one in tab Abap Object Search (DevEpos) and within Tags, too:

I'm running Eclipse 2024-06 and those plugin versions

S/4 Hana on-premise is version SAP-Basis 7.57 SP2.

Do I miss the obvious? Anything I've got to upgrade or any switches I've to enable?

entry.title: Re: Converting ICON value to Image in CDS View
entry.content: The annotation proposed for the metadata extension has no effect on the Fiori Elements application. The text is still displayed in the field.

entry.title: Re: Travel expenses payment in foreign currency - PR05
entry.content:
The problem is solved. 
Although the refund amount is displayed in GBP, the billing is done in the original currency of the receipt.
You need to display additional columns (Amount in doc.curr. and Document Currency) in transaction FBL1N to see this.

entry.title: Insufficient permissions to access the requested data
entry.content:
While hitting "profile/identity/v4.1/Users/.search" API in Postman getting below error:

{
    "status": 401,
    "schemas": [
        "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:2.0:Error",
        "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error"
    ],
    "urn:ietf:params:scim:api:messages:concur:2.0:Error": {
        "messages": [
            {
                "code": "UNAUTHORIZED"
            }
        ]
    },
    "detail": "Insufficient permissions to access the requested data"
}
Need help to resolve this issue, thanks

entry.title: Re: sap.fe.macros.Field - How do i get the base control (...
entry.content: Yes that's the use case i tried on the linked page and that works well, the value state gets removed once a proper value is defined.

entry.title: Re: Table maintenance generator in SAP ABAP - only allow new entries and delete entries
entry.content: Little typo: you have created a table maintenance dialog via the table maintenance generator.

entry.title: Re: Deletion of one employee number

entry.title: Re: S4 Public Cloud - Standard order types - Rush Order t...
entry.content:
Hi,

in both cases the sales order types as known from ECC are not available.

In case of Cash Sales please vote for this Customer Influence Request.

https://influence.sap.com/sap/ino/#/idea/289389

Here you will get an update after CE2408 how to build a Cash Sales solution in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition.

Regards,

Doreen

entry.title: Re: Questions regarding the distinctions between SAP HCM on-premise and H4S4:
entry.content: Removing "SAP S/4HANA Cloud for Human Resources" tag as this seems to be an On-Premise topic.

entry.title: Future Hire /Current hire notification to manager
entry.content:
Team, 

Requirement is to trigger a custom notification on hire date of employee.

In EC if you add employee it is working fine,however when candidate is pushed from Manage pending hire to EC and submitted, no notification trigger

Business rule Screenshot is attached.- Rule is placed on Save alert under job info. base object Job information
Workflow screenshot is attached

Note form EC side working fine, From Manage pending hire if candidate comes it is not triggering email notification

Kindly help

entry.title: Re: Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@RossR in my blog I am not assigning labels within the HwD view itself. In fact, I'm not assigning any texts in my basic example. In the advanced (next) blog on modeling, I associate separate dimensions that again associate a text view. 

I've reproduced your setup and my conclusions:

  • In your screenshots, it seems that you are not using your Node Type Values in the right way. You have selected column NODETYPE as containing the types, but these types you are not using in the "Node Type Value" definition. E.g., for CHILDID "00000001" your NODETYPE='0HIER...', but you are not using that node type in your HwD definition. There you use 'Text Node' as the node type value, but I do not see that value in your NODETYPE column. If you fix this, you might actually see at least the selected columns (e.g. what you have selected under "Column 1") as your description.
  • I would argue that your method of assigning labels within the HwD view, is not supported. Instead, work according to the setup in my blog. That means:
    • From the dimension to which you associate the HwD view (Product in my examples), associate the text view for the leaf nodes 
    • From the HwD view, associate a dimension with itself an associated text view to the intermediary nodes. You might not need the dimension and directly associate a text view to the HwD view.

entry.title: Re: CDS view to find the Classification characteristic de...
entry.content:
Hi Gahan ,

 

Please use the CDS view ClfnCharcDescForKeyDate to retrieve the description of the characteristic shown

 

Best Regards

Enda

entry.title: Re: sap.fe.macros.Field - How do i get the base control (...
entry.content: @nicolas_lunet I see you're doing it for a dropdown. But what if it's for a field with a value help (not dropdown via annotations) and you want to clear the field to clear (blank) and not set to any value from the value help? That's the use case I'm after

entry.title: Re: No check on foreign key in ADT-defined database table
entry.content:
Value Helps in RAP are defined with Consumption.ValueHelpDefinition annotations as described here: Providing Value Help for the Selection Fields | SAP Help Portal

Foreign key relationships are evaluated by the RAP framework, for example, during a read-by-association operations and in the ON condition of associations.

entry.title: Re: payment difference posted to an expense account as re...
entry.content: @sofianos_g，Yes, removing values of tax category in the account - 71000010 Indeed solved my issue! The previous value was set as “- input tax allowed”, so it derived the tax code from opened invoice

entry.title: Active Directory and SAP S/4 integration via BTP
entry.content:
Dear all
one of my customer is requesting me the possibility to interface is Active Directory with his SAP S/4. And he wants to create SAP users in a specific group in AC to be created in SAP S/4.
and even deleted or set to end of validity once the user went out of this AD group
there's a BTP service that allows this kind of scenario?

entry.title: Re: TECO status
entry.content:
Hi Patil.

When you apply TECO system status the reservation created from the material component is closed, meaning the requirement no longer exists. However, you can still manually post goods issue. You can also reverse the TECO status so that the requirement will be 'reopened,' and the reservation can be used again.

Regards
Marcio Blos

entry.title: can we prevent DIR versioninng for DIR status is released
entry.content:
Dears,

 

we need to prevent create version if the dir status is released

is it possible?

Regards,,

Abdulfattah Hussein

entry.title: Re: BAPI_ACC_DOCUMENT_POST Error G/L account is not defin...
entry.content:
Why are you not populating criteria parameter of BAPI.

DATA: Lw_CRITERIA TYPE BAPIACKEC9.

Lw_CRITERIA-ITEMNO_ACC = P_LINES.

* Customer

Lw_CRITERIA-FIELDNAME = 'KNDNR'.

Lw_CRITERIA-CHARACTER = P_VBAK-KUNNR.

APPEND Lw_CRITERIA TO P_CRITERIA.

* Material

Lw_CRITERIA-FIELDNAME = 'ARTNR'.

Lw_CRITERIA-CHARACTER = P_VBAP-MATNR.

APPEND Lw_CRITERIA TO P_CRITERIA.

This way populate all fields which you are passing in line item

entry.title: Disable Object Page in List Report Developed in SAP Cloud Abap Development Enviornment
entry.content:
I have created a list report page on the first page it shows data in tabular format, but when I click on any of the lines it takes me to the inside object page but I don't need an object page in this report. 

How can I achieve this using Eclipse abap development?

I really appreciate any help you can provide. 

Screenshot's are attached below.

entry.title: Re: Accept/Reject Operations in SAM while EAM_PHASE_MODEL...
entry.content: User login as technician. And the Accept/Reject mobile status has been configured in Backend system as well as Syclo configuration.

entry.title: Re: Carry Forward - deletes the first opening month - SAC

entry.title: Re: Accept/Reject Operations in SAM while EAM_PHASE_MODEL" enabled
entry.content: Yes Prathik, User login as technician. And the Accept/Reject mobile status has been configured in Backend system as well as Syclo configuration.

entry.title: Re: List binding to input of UI5 task as approval form not working
entry.content: Hi @DianaCr. Was you able to resolve the issue. I am also facing the same issue.

entry.title: Re: Accept/Reject Operations in SAM while EAM_PHASE_MODEL" enabled
entry.content:
Below are the requirement - 

Requirement is to have two additional buttons for accept and reject in operation details screen. In case technician rejects, an additional screen should appear to select reason of rejection. 

Actions in SSAM should trigger operation user status update in S/4 and FSM. For this a new operation user status profile is created 

Requirement for both Accept/Reject buttons 

Accept  

Whenever technician accepts the order operation in SSAM User status “Accept” should be updated against the operation in S/4, This user status should be sent to FSM,.Dispatcher can view “Accept” against the operation in FSM.  Phase and Sub phase should remain as it is i.e. Execution and Ready for Execution. 

Development Approach: 

We are currently working on updating the User Status in the PMMobileStatus entity while keeping the Mobile status unchanged. Here are the results: 

In MDK development for accept and reject we are referring “PMMobileStatus” entity and “UserStatus” property. And below details we are trying to update. 

When User accept operation – ACPD 

In mdk debugging we are getting initial value of UserStatus property (“PMMobileStatus” entity) in assigned operation, whatever available in backend. 

We are performing update entity action, in local (before sync) it’s updating the userstatus accept/reject properly. But while sync we are facing below issues. 

  1. Update UserStatus only from SAM - Sync Failed and transaction error is “Mobile Status is mandatory”.
  2. Update existing Mobile Status (Ready) & UserStatus from SAM App - Sync failed, and transaction error is “Mobile Status ‘Ready’ not supported”. 
  3.  Update Mobile Status & User Status from SAM App - Sync Successful, In backend Only Mobile status updating but not user status. And in trace we can see the record of UserStatus as well as MobileStatus as mentioned in below snapshot.

entry.title: Re: Table maintenance generator in SAP ABAP - only allow new entries and delete entries
entry.content: Thanks. Modified the post

entry.title: Re: Fiori catalog doesnt appear in Launchpad after transport
entry.content: This was very helpful

entry.title: SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q2-24 Updates & Highlights
entry.content:
It has been a busy last quarter for all of us in SAP Sustainability! Besides delivering innovative new features and functions in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, we also had a strong presence at SAP Sapphire and have exciting success stories to share. This blog post continues my quarterly series and gives you an overview of some of the highlights. I’m covering:

  • Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculation
  • Corporate Balance Analytics
  • Emission Factor Mapping with AI
  • Spend-based Calculation for Scope 3.1
  • Other Release Highlights
  • SAP Sapphire 2024 Recap
  • Customer & Analyst Update

 

Corporate Carbon Footprint Calculation

In my last blog post (SAP Sustainability Footprint Management: Q1-24 Updates & Highlights), I already introduced our new form-based data collection approach, calculating Waste Generated in Operations. We have now enhanced this by Business Activities for any Greenhouse Gas (GHG) category, enabling you the calculation of a full Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) as per the GHG Protocol. You can set up those Business Activities at plant level by specifying the calculation method and emission factor for the footprint calculation. Business Users in your organization can then manually input activity data for these activities in the relevant apps. This data, combined with information from other sources, can be included in your overall footprint calculations and displayed in the analytical apps. This new approach provides you with maximum flexibility by enabling you to add data that isn’t available in your ERP system and to choose between activity- and spend-based calculations. It also speeds up the initial setup process and provides you with the possibility to start with the form-based data collection. You can then move to an ERP-centric collection over time for specific GHG scopes and categories, or also for specific plants in your company.

To set up the data collection, first ensure that the user roles are assigned correctly, and then configure the Footprint Inventory Scope. In the Manage Footprint Inventory Scopes app, edit an existing footprint inventory scope or create a new one, assign companies and plants, activate the appropriate GHG Data Form, and provide a guidance text for data entry. After you’ve done this, the form-based data collection will be enabled.

In the next step, you can create Business Activities for which you want to collect data. A Business Activity can be any activity in your company that you want to calculate emissions for, such as different types of travel (air, train, car) for various locations. Business users can then input relevant data (e.g., fuel used or distance traveled), and the system calculates emissions based on provided emission factors. Those can be updated over time by creating new emission factor periods within each activity. In the Manage Business Activities app, you can create a new activity by entering information such as the GHG Category and validity period. Here you also indicate the Preferred Footprint Type, defining which kind of data the business user later should input:

  • Manual Emission: Direct input of total CO2e values without needing to calculate or provide additional calculation data
  • Quantity-Based: Quantity data (e.g., material produced or distance traveled) to calculate CO2e
  • Spend-Based: Amount of money spent on an activity to determine CO2e

Finally, you need to specify the calculation method (e.g. distance- or fuel-based) and respective emission factor(s) by either entering it manually or selecting a reference from an LCA dataset. The system then calculates the total CO2e accordingly.

Manage Business Activities

After successful setup of the Business Activities, you can create GHG data collections. In the Manage GHG Data Collections app, those can be set up for each footprint inventory scope, period, plant, and business activity. The Data Collections can also be regenerated if there are changes in the Business Activities.

Now, the system is ready for the business users in your company to enter data in forms in the collection apps. In the Record GHG Data app, by default all relevant plants and periods are displayed. It's possible to exclude any unnecessary plants by marking them as Not Applicable. After selecting the desired plant, users can enter data – quantity, amount spend, or total CO2e – following the Preferred Footprint Type to ensure accuracy. Alternatively, data can be imported using the provided template. If the entered value differs by more than 30% from the previous period, the system flags this with a warning message.

GHG Data Form

In the final step, check data readiness and calculate footprints. In the Calculate Footprints app, you can as usual trigger the footprint calculation. If you’re using only the form-based data collection in the selected inventory scope, there’s no need to enter additional data. The progress and completion status is visible in the Data Readiness tab.

For more information on the different steps, check the scenario guide Form-Based Data Collection for Business Activities on SAP Help.

For a detailed guidance on setting up the solution for calculating corporate carbon footprints, check our brand-new Corporate Carbon Footprint (CCF) Implementation Guide. This package includes sample data for a model company and an implementation book to do an end-to-end setup of SAP Sustainability Footprint Management for calculating the CCF of the example company. Note that the target audience is consultants and partners, and access is restricted, requiring a login via your SAP account.

 

Corporate Balance Analytics

For analyzing the calculated corporate carbon footprint and the corporate balance, we released a new Sankey chart to visualize the emissions across a reporting period. This chart helps you to understand the major contributors for the emissions. Identifying these hotspots enables you to define specific actions for reducing the emissions in those areas and by that supporting your company’s decarbonization journey. The chart can be accessed directly via the Corporate Balance app or by clicking on the respective card in the Footprint Overview app.

Corporate Balance

You might have seen such a multilevel Sankey chart when visualizing a company’s financial balance and the respective financial flows. In SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, the company’s total CO2e emissions are displayed at the center, on the left side you see the input emissions from the different GHG scopes and categories, and you can follow their flow through the company. On the right, the proportions of the carbon emissions distributed among your various product and non-product outputs are visualized. The widths of the respective flows are proportional to their quantity. You can select a specific item subtype in the Sankey chart to display the breakdown of the item subtype into all the individual items in the table below the chart. The table is sorted based on the value of the emissions in descending order to help you gain insights. For example, clicking on 3.1 – Purchased Goods and Services will show you the emissions for all your purchased (raw) materials.

Via the filters in the header, you can select the Footprint Inventory Scope you want to analyze, and you can also filter on specific companies or plants. Furthermore, it’s possible to select one or more Footprint Inventories, enabling you to display the emissions across a reporting period. This way you can, for example, analyze your corporate footprint for the full (financial) year. Check SAP Help for more information: Using the Corporate Balance Application.

 

Emission Factor Mapping with AI

At SAP Sapphire 2024, we officially announced our first released feature in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management that is powered by SAP Business AI! It’s now possible to leverage Artificial Intelligence for mapping emission factors to your purchased products, reducing your manual effort of mapping ERP and Lifecycle Assessment (LCA) data and by that saving you significant time. The following demo video gives you an impression of the powerful capabilities and how your processes are improved and simplified (click on the picture to start the video):

Emission Factor Mapping with AI

For the mapping, we use the OpenAI text-embedding-ada-002 model. Embeddings are vector representations of textual data providing context and semantic meaning of the text. First, we generate embeddings for emission factors from LCA databases and store them. When mapping a product, an embedding is generated from the product’s data coming from the ERP system, currently comprising product and product group name and description. We plan to extend that to more dimensions, such as geography and commodity code in future releases. The generated embeddings from the LCA and ERP data are stored in the HANA Cloud Vector Engine. In the next step, the system compares them for similarity using cosine distance and calculates the Similarity Score. The closest 10 are provided as suggested results. The calculated score helps in identifying the quality of the mapping. In the future, we also plan to enhance this score by additional variables to make it more representative of the data and more user-friendly.

The feature is included in the Manage Purchased Products Footprint app, which went through a redesign for this purpose. You now find separate tabs for Products without Footprints, containing both entries from master and transactional data, and Product Groups without Footprints. From the respective list, you can select those products (groups) you would like to map, clicking on Generate Mappings triggers the process. Go to the Unreleased Footprints tab to view the mapping results. Here you also find the Similarity Score, clicking on a line item opens the details and the alternative footprint suggestions. After reviewing and releasing your preferred mappings, the footprints are moved to the Released Footprints tab. For auditing purposes, the detail pages of the footprints are also including the information if AI was used for the mapping. If yes, the Similarity Score is stored, too. The AI mapping works for all LCA datasets maintained int the Manage Emission Factors app. When you upload a new data package, the embeddings will be generated automatically. More information can be found on SAP Help: Using AI to Map Purchased Products and Purchased Product Groups.

Of course, it’s also possible to map your products and product groups manually, and with the revamped design of the app it’s now even easier than before. In the respective tab, click on Assign Manually and either download/ import the template, or select the entries you want to map and click on Map Manually. See SAP Help for more information: Manually Map Purchased Products and Product Groups without Footprints. Alternatively, you can also still create a product (group) footprint directly in the Released Footprints tab by clicking on the Create button or by importing the provided template. For all details, see Manage Purchased Product Footprints in SAP Help.

With the mapping on Product Group level, we provide a backup for products for which you don’t have granular emission factors available or also for products that have similar or even the same emission factors. Thus, you save more time and effort in mapping ERP to LCA data. In the Products without Footprints tab, you can see in the column Product Group Emission Factor Reference if there’s already an emission factor assigned that would be applied as a backup and if so, which one. In the Manage Calculation Variants app, this is now included as a new footprint determination method that can be applied to organizational footprint inventory calculations.

Note that with the release of the new emission factors mapping features we have also deprecated the Manage Mappings app and will remove it from the SAP Fiori launchpad in October 2024. In the Manage Purchased Product Footprints app, you can import the mappings to your footprints.

 

Spend-based Calculation for Scope 3.1

Besides activity-based emission factors, it’s now also possible to map spend-based emission factors to purchased products to calculate your GHG Scope 3.1 (Purchased Goods and Services) emissions as per the spend-based calculation method. The specified spend-based purchased product footprints are used in all determination methods to calculate the CO2e footprint value of Goods Receipt from Supplier, provided the amount and currency are imported. The mapping can be done for both products and product groups. And you can do this mapping either manually or leverage the new AI capabilities. For more details see SAP Help: Manage Purchased Product Footprints.

The required spend-based emission factors can be imported and managed the usual way via the Manage Emission Factors app. The respective Excel-template has been enhanced accordingly, so currency information can be added. Besides uploading own factors, you can also use the Supply Chain Greenhouse Gas Emission Factors v1.2 by NAICS-6 dataset, provided by the United States Environmental Protection Agency (EPA). This data package is available as default content in the app. For more information, see Using the Manage Emission Factors Application in SAP Help.

 

Other Release Highlights

For calculating the emissions of the manufacturing process (GHG Scope 1 & 2) in a specific plant and allocate them to the manufactured products, it was always required to set up an Energy Flow Model and input energy bill data. Now, we provide the additional option to use only Planned Energy Consumption Rates for calculating energy consumption and distribution. In the Manage Calculation Variants app, you can set up a calculation variant to use the option Use Only Planned Consumption Rate. The system then projects the monthly energy needs of each product by using the imported Internal Service Confirmation business transactions to match energy sources to their planned consumption rates, specified in the Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates. The energy source footprint items are connected to the resources with the planned quantity as the actual quantity, and on the energy sources the sum of planned quantities is evaluated with the emission factor of the energy sources to determine the CO2e footprint. More details can be found in SAP Help Portal: Using the Manage Calculation Variants Application and Using the Manage Planned Energy Consumption Rates Application.

To further improve the User Experience, we provided additional file template enhancements. This includes the templates for master and business transaction data, which now provide field descriptions for better understanding. For an easier way of uploading emission factors, we’re now providing a new Simple Template in the Manage Emission Factors app. This compact version contains essential information such as name and validity period and is intended particularly to upload basic data for testing purposes or simple use cases. For more information, see SAP Help: Using the Manage Emission Factors Application.

We also enhanced the APIs available on SAP Business Accelerator Hub. They’ve been updated to support the technical specifications for PCF data exchange version 2.1.0.

Besides the Europe (Netherlands) region under Microsoft Azure (cf-eu20), SAP Sustainability Footprint Management is now also available in two additional regions:

  • US East (VA) under Amazon Web Services (AWS) (cf-us10).
  • Singapore under Amazon Web Services (AWS) (cf-ap11).

You can select one of these regions when creating a subaccount. For more information, see Setting Up the Global Account and Subaccount in SAP Help.

For collecting actual emissions for purchased products directly from your suppliers, you can now also leverage the SAP Ariba Sourcing integration. Carbon footprint data is gathered from suppliers using the Carbon Footprint Questionnaire. This dynamic tool allows suppliers to provide a versatile range of responses based on industry-standard values, ensuring accuracy and compliance with environmental regulations. Once suppliers are awarded, their carbon footprint data is seamlessly transferred from Ariba Sourcing through an automated scheduled task, enhancing efficiency and data integrity. Received in Sustainability Footprint Management, the footprints can be reviewed in the Manage Supplier Footprints app. After releasing, they’re available in the footprint calculation. Find all details on this integration and how to set it up on SAP Help: SAP Ariba Sourcing Integration with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management.

The following video nicely demonstrates the user flows and the integration of the two products (click on the picture to start the video):

SAP Ariba Sourcing integration

 

SAP Sapphire 2024 Recap

At this year’s SAP Sapphire, Sustainability had again a strong presence, including many on-stage sessions featuring SAP executives and experts, as well as customers and partners. And also our demo booth in Orlando and Barcelona was much-frequented! At the event, we announced a set of sustainability tracking, monitoring and reporting capabilities, one piece of it being the above-described new carbon calculator engine for corporate footprints in SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. Those new capabilities are intended to help organizations steer their sustainability commitments and keep pace with global regulations, built on our ERP-centric, cloud-based, AI-enabled approach. Learn more in the article AI-Enabled Sustainability at the Heart of ERP by Sophia Mendelsohn, Chief Sustainability and Commercial Officer at SAP.

Did you miss the event? No worries! The recordings of some of the sessions and demos are still available on the Virtual Channel, check out the Sustainability Management track here: SAP Sapphire 2024 Session Catalog.

If you want to discover our AI-powered SAP solutions for calculating and reporting corporate and product carbon footprints, watch the below demo recording by Jim Sullivan, Head of Product Management for SAP Sustainability. He’s showcasing the ERP-centric data collection and calculations on the path towards a green ledger (click on the picture to start the video):

 

Customer & Analyst Update

One highlight of the past quarter was our customer Martur Fompak International, one of the world’s leading suppliers of automotive seat systems, winning the SAP Innovation Award 2024 in two categories: Industry Leader and Sustainability Hero! They implemented SAP Sustainability Footprint Management to incorporate carbon emission values into the equation for every decision they take to steer the industry and the world towards a more sustainable future. They’re further revolutionizing cockpit customization with their AI-driven innovation, applying several SAP solutions. When personalizing cockpit elements such as seats, door panels, and dashboards, OEMs and customers can now see the carbon impact of each choice in real time. By providing and promoting more sustainable and environmentally friendly options, Martur Fompak has decreased carbon emissions by 34% for each seat the company produces. Read the full story here: SAP Innovation Award 2024: Martur Fompak International.

Another customer who is adopting SAP Sustainability Footprint Management is CP Foods. The leading food producer, serving over 4 billion people globally, is adopting multiple SAP solutions to enhance its digital transformation and sustainability initiatives. They aim to achieve substantial reductions in carbon emissions by 2030 and 2050 and have set ambitious Net Zero goals validated by the Science-Based Targets Initiative. The company will use SAP's technology to monitor, report, and manage sustainability data in real time, aiding compliance with future carbon regulations and enhancing operational efficiency. For more information, check the press release: CP Foods Selects SAP Solutions for Cloud Sustainability.

A great endorsement of our products and strategy comes from the analysts: SAP has been named a leader among 18 vendors in the first-ever IDC MarketScape: Worldwide Carbon Accounting and Management Applications 2024 Vendor Assessment. As carbon management evolves, they confirmed that SAP stands out with our solutions like SAP Sustainability Footprint Management and SAP Green Ledger. Our tools integrate sustainability into business processes, helping organizations meet regulatory requirements, enhance operational efficiency, and make more informed decisions. The IDC report emphasizes the importance of data integration and advanced tools for effective carbon management, positioning SAP’s comprehensive suite as a critical asset for businesses facing escalating environmental scrutiny. Read the press release here: SAP in IDC MarketScape Carbon Accounting & Management.

 

Additional Information

For in-depth details on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, including concepts, setup, and the application help, I recommend you browsing through the SAP Help Portal. For an overview of all new features and functions that have been released, check the What’s New section, which is updated with every new software release.

To get an outlook on the features and functions that are planned to be added to the solution in the next months, have a look into SAP Road Map Explorer. This is updated regularly and will provide you with an overview of all planned innovations and which benefits those will bring to you.

For exploring and experimenting in a live system, register for the free SAP Sustainability Footprint Management Trial. You’ll receive an e-mail with access information within seconds after your registration and can then test the solution at your own convenience within a 30-days trial period. It also includes a wide range of guided tours designed to help you navigate through all the features and functions comprehensively. Recently, we also added new tours and working on additional ones at the moment, representing the latest features.

If you want to learn more about the product, check out our SAP Learning Journey: Taking Action on Climate Change with SAP Sustainability Footprint Management. This eLearning includes three comprehensive courses, each consisting of multiple units. The training is interactive, featuring numerous hands-on simulations and exercises. Upon successful completion of the program, you’ll be rewarded with a Record of Achievement and a digital badge. We also plan a major update in the upcoming months to provide separate learning journeys for different roles and levels, stay tuned!

To stay up to date on SAP Sustainability Footprint Management, follow me here in the SAP Community. In regular cadence, I’ll publish blog posts giving you updates on all the product’s new features and functions as well as deep dives into the product capabilities. I’m also looking forward to engaging with you directly, feel free to leave your feedback in the comment section.

Priya

Working when full authorization given on UDT and UDO registrations under Customization, but working for others users with No authorization.

Tried copying authorization of working users, still same issue.

Attached error screenshot for reference.

SAP Business One

Mark

entry.title: Re: Solution for US Payapp in construction
entry.content:
Historically many people handled the AIA billing process offline and then entered the invoice in SAP after it was fully approved.  Others have built AIA like output forms that print from SAP.  A few years ago SAP partner LTIMindtree built a BTP solution to address the complexities of construction billing, including interaction with subcontractors, pencil copy billing and approval processes, and output forms.  This solution is available in the SAP store.  Project Paychain | LTIMindtree Limited (sap.com)   

SAP partner Smartapp also has billing capabilities in their SmartappOne solution which is also available in the SAP store.  This solution also includes jobsite execution functionality like lean project scheduling, document control, jobsite safety, and more with out of the box integration to S/4Cloud public edition. This solution is also available on the SAPstore.  Smartapp ONE™ | Smartapp.com, Inc. (sap.com) 

I hope that helps.

entry.title: Become an AI early adopter of the generative AI hub (via SAP AI Core) - Last chance (Jul 31st)!
entry.content:
Drawing pictures and writing blog posts has never been as easy as today. With large language models seemingly everything got possible, or at least so much easier. Also in the business sphere many new opportunities turned up and showed us: we are just at the beginning of a new era. 

I personally remember the first experiments I did with ChatGPT - very impressed by its capabilities used in my private life (like having a personal kitchen manager, telling me what to cook with what I have left), I started seeing more and more cases in my professional life as well. So many business processes that can be made so much more efficient, by "just" adding a large language model to it. 

With #documentinformationextraction I got the very first touch on how SAP can bring GenAI to the business user. With Document Information Extraction you have a tool (either with an own UI, or via API) which allows you to extract unstructured data from documents (PDFs or jpgs for instance) and put the extracted data into an SAP friendly table format. With GenAI it almost did not matter anymore, which type of document or which language - it just worked (with the right prompts). 

You can try it out today via the free trial on the #businesstechnologyplatform! (Link to the discovery center)
Did you already try it out, or have it productive already? Let us know if you are missing something! (Link to Continuous influence channel)

Extracting data from documents is an important efficiency gain in day to day business. But we want to offer more, we want to bring the flexibility of using large language models to our customers. Allowing you to build your own use cases, in your specific environments. With the generative AI hub on SAP AI Core you can build your own GenAI use cases, with your SAP data, within your safe environment of the SAP BTP. You might be wondering which LLMs we offer you - well, quite a few, top notch models like chatGPT, mistral, etc. (check here for most recent infos)!

I bet you have already use cases in mind that you would want to try out!

Curious? Then sign up to the Early Adopter Care Program right away and benefit from a quick onboarding and getting your own use case live with the expertise of our product team. 
This program is running until July 31st - so don't miss out and register right away. 
You do not need much, just an idea of where you want to try out generative AI capabilities, provided via the generative AI hub. 

Do you already have experience with the generative AI hub on SAP AI Core? 
Did you already try it out, or have it productive already? Let us know if you are missing something! (Link to Continuous influence channel)

Artificial Intelligence is here to stay - the best you can do is to get familiar with it from day 1 and use it in your best interest. Let's learn to walk together with the help of such programs as the early adopter care.

Check also out the Joule Early Adopter Care Program: Link to EAC for Joule in SAP Start with SuccessFactors and SAP S/4HANA Cloud, public edition.

 

More Infos:

  • WHAT IS THE GENERATIVE AI HUB ALL ABOUT? 
  • WHO IS JOULE?
  • Roadmap of (Gen)AI at SAP
  • What are foundation models all about?

entry.title: Hi, I have few doubts around CBC:
entry.content:
1. During setting up of PE(Evaluation) in Starter System, I have learned that the Starter System Org Data is preconfigured with the Org Data mentioned in the Process Flow -Test Scripts(Signavio Process Nav.) for the ease of performing Fit To Standard Workshops. But is it possible to set up own set of Master Data(This is Client Request) as Org Data in Starter System?


2. As a Lead Configuration Expert/LOB Experts, the primary responsibility would be to ensure the phases/milestone are completed for the PE assigned to the Customizing Tenant(100) of the Starter/Development System. Do we need to perform the same activities(incl. Product Specific Configuration) for the PE assigned to the Development Tenant(80). If so why?

SAP Central Business Configuration SAP S/4HANA

Thanks.

entry.title: Re: Phase-Based Maintenance Process With Lean Services in SAP S4 HANA Asset Management.
entry.content:
Hi , 

For definition of lean services , please refer to note https://me.sap.com/notes/3227060/E.

Its incorrect to use the word Small , as stated by you "Lean services are intended for the procurement of small and highly specialized services .." 

Lean Services and Enhanced Limits can handle services of any value and complexity(using item hierarchies) . You can consider changing the text to ensure customers have a right understanding.

Regards,

Amit

entry.title: Re: Digital Skills Initiative Applicant Guide (For self-study candidates)
entry.content:
Hi @David_Chaviano 

My name is David Gachemi, a recent graduate from Kenya having pursued BSc. Industrial Chemistry. I would like to appreciate the establishment of such a good idea of enabling deserving individuals to get digital skills which are certifiable. I am a youth in my mid 20’s who is a first-generation college student in my family. I am seeking a certification of SAP Business One which will enable me to handle the current market opportunities present in Kenya and outside. Currently in Kenya, there has been a misconception that SAP is for large companies whereas almost 70% of SAP customers fall under the SME category. This directly implies that SAP is and can work in SMEs. With the use of SAP Business One Certification, I can convince clients to be confident in my implementation of SAP Business One and also increase my chances of getting a job opportunity. Several SMEs have been opened in Kenya which presents a unique opportunity, especially with the troublesome taxation in the country a chance to use a business process software that automates most of the processes.

At the moment, I am unable to finance myself to do the certification due to a low salary that I currently receive. Currently, I can do the course without any challenges. I will be deeply grateful to be part of this program. Please consider my application.

Kind Regards,

David Gachemi

entry.title: Workbook for users to lock data (version and year)
entry.content:
Hello everyone,

I'm in the process of implementing/creating a workbook to enable users to lock data based on the version and year. I've based my test implementation on this post although we're on SAP BW on HANA with standard BPC. I don't quite achieve the same results. I'm also unsure whether this is the correct approach or not. The idéa is to use a workbook to manage this process.

My implementation so far is to create a new aDSO to store the information with the relevant InfoObjects:

- ZLOCK (key-figure to put in 1 or 0 for lock/unlock)
- ZPYEAR (to choose the year)
- 0VERSION (to choose the version)

An aggregation level is then created on top of the new aDSO, where a query is the created on top of the aggregation level. I've also created a filter, two planning functions and two sequences for locking and unlocking.

Planning function 0 = unlock 1 = lock:

I've created a new class (based on CL_*_DS_EXIT) and changed the IS_PROCTECTED:

I then activate the dataslice in the relevant aDSO.

The idéa is that the user opens a workbook and chooses the year in the prompt. It will then show all the versions for that year and a button will be created to run a lock and unlock planning sequence.

E.g. For the year 2024, the version P1 should be locked. The user would then click on the "Lock" button, where it will put a "1" in the highlighted cell.

My problem right now is that the planning function executes with no error, but I can't save the change to the aDSO.

1. Is there a guide somewhere which goes into details on how to proceed or implement such a workbook?
2. Am I missing something more in the code?
3. The workbook should be able to handle multiple locks, so let's say the user wants to lock version P1, P3 and P4 for 2020, but I'm unsure on how to do this? I'm assuming I need to created the cell ranges with those information, but I can't seem to find out where to find information.

Any help is greatly appreciated or direction to a similar posts. I've searched the web, but unable to find something which is as "simple" as the one I linked above, althought I still can't succesfully implement it

entry.title: Re: SAP® Extended Warehouse Management in SAP S/4HANA 2022 – What’s New

entry.title: Re: How to schedule a SAP Certification exam on learning.sap.com
entry.content:
Hi @Stella ,

You need an additional exam attempt on your account to try again.

entry.title: How to create a full-text index from ZIP or RAR files in S/4HANA to perform a full-text search
entry.content:
Dear All,

In our S/4HANA system we create a full-text index after uploading documents (PDF, Word files, ZIP files) and via this full-text index we perform a full-text search. According to the "SAP HANA Search Developer Guide (2017)" the ZIP / RAR files are not supported when creating a full-text index (see excerpt below) which we also tested and that is the case (a full-text search in the ZIP files does not work).

In the meantime (since 2017), does anybody know a solution how to resolve this??

Excerpt from the Developer Guide:
"In a SAP HANA database, when you create a table that contains large text or binary documents, you can define the columns with the TEXT or BINTEXT data type. This data type allows you to query textual data and present content excerpts in search hit lists. You can also reconstruct the document and display its original textual content.

Note: Embedded document filters can automatically extract text and metadata from most popular document formats, including Adobe PDF, Microsoft Office, OpenDocument, email, HTML, XML, and plain text.
Archive file formats, which may contain nested sub-files, are not supported. This includes ZIP and RAR files, as well as personal email folders like PST files."

Thank you in advance for your help / tips.

Best regards, Stan

entry.title: Created accrual item types cannot be selected in accrual account determination config.(public cloud)
entry.content:
Hi everyone,

We have a question related purchase order accruals configuration.

We have created Z* accrual item types. When we try to assign different accounts for each different accrual item type in PER_ACCR_ACCNT field, we realised we cannot see these newly created Z* accrual item types in Accrual Item Type field. There are only standard accrual item types.

Is it standard behaviour of public cloud and are we not able to use Z* accrual item types?

entry.title: Why is the 'match' function not working on a String in my RPA project?
Re: Signavio Process Manager: RACI Matrix, Role Management and pointers about "A", "C
entry.content:
Very good article which explains a lot about RACI in SAP Signavio Process Models. I would have one question as something was not clear to me. In the article you say this : 

"Notice the diagram has 1 Org Pool, 3 Role Lanes and one black-box pool / external pool for the Custome"

However in BPM Diagram 2.0 under Diagram Element Types I can not find object such as Org.Pool or Black-box pool/external pool. There is only Pool or Colapsed Pool. Can you please explain ?

entry.title: Migration of Legacy Data to SAP Public Cloud: An Overview and Key Questions
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

We are currently facing the challenge of migrating legacy data to the SAP Cloud, Public Edition. We know and also recommend to our client to not migrate legacy data in SAP Public Cloud for Controlling purposes. Our goal is to migrate values up to the end of the fiscal year 2023 and subsequently capture monthly P&L values for controlling purposes. In this context, we have several specific questions regarding the migration process that we would like to share and discuss with you:

Question 1: Is it possible to migrate monthly values in SAP Public Cloud?

We want to ensure that our monthly values can be migrated correctly and completely to the SAP Public Cloud. Are there any best practices or specific tools that can assist us in this process?

Question 2: General and subsidiary ledgers in SAP must always be reconciled - does this mean that all objects need to be migrated monthly?

An important question for us is whether it is necessary to migrate all objects monthly to ensure that the general and subsidiary ledgers are always reconciled. Are there specific requirements or best practices we should follow in this regard?

Question 3: Migration of the asset subledger: Should this be migrated as of 31.12.23, and then all asset movements for 2024 be booked in SAP?

Our consideration is to migrate the asset subledger as of December 31, 2023, and subsequently book all asset movements for the year 2024 in SAP. Is this approach sensible, and are there any specific challenges we should be aware of?

Question 4: Can theoretically only P&L values be migrated monthly on their own?

Another point we would like to discuss is the possibility of migrating only the P&L values monthly, without considering other data. Is this feasible and advisable in the SAP Public Cloud?

Question 5: What options are available in SAP Public Cloud Controlling Apps or functionalities to migrate or upload these legacy data for CO comparisons with the previous year?

We are looking for effective solutions to migrate or upload our legacy data for CO comparisons with the previous year. What Controlling Apps or functionalities does the SAP Public Cloud offer to achieve this?

Question 6: What options does the customer have in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to analyze their legacy data on a monthly basis for CO purposes?

Lastly, we are interested in understanding the options available in SAP Analytics Cloud (SAC) to analyze legacy data on a monthly basis for CO purposes. What tools or functionalities can be utilized to create detailed analyses and reports?

We look forward to your experiences, tips, and best practices on these topics and are eager for a constructive discussion!

Best regards,

Mara

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 for HANA Email Alerts showing a common Subject
entry.content:
Hi juzersm1990

If you want to change the default template "SAP Business One mail message, the subject is in the message body" when you send out email with the subject empty, 

The functionality asked by you, looks like not supported.

Thank You.

Kind Regards
Nidhi

entry.title: Approval procedure using Query for 2 conditions UDF and Price
entry.content:
Dear Team,

We need to trigger approval for PO based on header Level UDF and Price combination we are using below query and its throwing error

Query:
SELECT Distinct 'TRUE' FROM OPOR where $[OPOR."U_EA_Item"]='Y' and $[OPOR."DocTotal"]<10000

Error:

Could not commit transaction: Error -1 detected during transaction

Please provide solution for this we need to have a approval procedure on 2 conditions at header level.

entry.title: AR - Open Items Migration in SAP Public Cloud: Interface Integration vs. Upload Template
entry.content:
Dear SAP Community,

As we continue to navigate the complexities of migrating our legacy data to the SAP Public Cloud, we have encountered a specific question regarding the migration of open items in the subledger. We are exploring whether it is possible to import open items using an interface instead of the standard upload template. Here are the key points we would like to discuss:

Is it possible to import subledger open items using an interface instead of the upload template?

We are considering the feasibility of using an interface to import subledger open items. Has anyone in the community successfully utilized an interface for this purpose, and if so, what were the benefits and challenges encountered?

Does the SAP Migration Cockpit accept interfaces for open items migration, or must the general and subledger always match using the migration templates?

We are also interested in understanding whether the SAP Migration Cockpit supports the use of interfaces for migrating open items. Is it mandatory for general and subledger data to always match using the predefined migration templates, or is there flexibility in the method of data import?

Understanding the best approach for migrating open items is crucial for the accuracy and integrity of our financial data. We appreciate any insights, experiences, or best practices you can share regarding the use of interfaces versus upload templates for this aspect of the migration process.

Thank you for your support and contributions to this discussion!

Best regards,

Mara

entry.title: Re: Is SAP Business One version 10.0 compatible with Wind...
entry.content:
Hello,

I did install the client, the server is Hana database which is installed on a linux machine and the complementary windows server is on a different machine.

So fare I only use it to read infos, not to write, so warranty is not an issue.

I would like to know if somebody use it in production mode (read/write).

entry.title: Re: Basic Withholding Tax (for customers) Process
entry.content:
Dear Customer,

Yes, you need to setup the withholding tax codes for customer side documents such that the incoming payments matches your A/R balances.

Steps:
Define WHT type for Payment posting
Define WHT code for the WHT type
Define a Condition type for WHT by Customers
Assign the Condition type to the WHT type
Make the necessary assignments such as WHT tax type to Company code, GL A/c determination etc.
Maintain the Condition type in SD Pricing procedure as statistical
Maintain the Condition record in VK11 with the Withholding tax code.
Maintain the tax code in Customer Master

Best Regards,
Debasis

entry.title: Re: Hi, I have few doubts around CBC:
entry.content:
Hi @anwesha08 ,

I'd like to provide information regarding your points mentioned.

1) The Starter Evaluation project is offering pre-delivered data and unfortunately it is not possible to change this. For more information please check below documents:

3395883 - Unable to avoid demo data in starter system

Systems and Demo Data | SAP Help Portal

Evaluation Project | SAP Help Portal 

2) Yes, project activities have to be repeated in each project created. However there is a function which makes it possible to Download Template Configuration:

Project Experience - Downloading Template Configuration

Downloading Your Business Configuration Adaptations in SAP Central Business Configuration

3453205 - "Prepare Download of My Configuration" button is not available in CBC Project

 

I hope this information helps, in case you have additionally questions please feel free to update this thread.

 

Kind regards,

Tamas

entry.title: Re: Hi, I have few doubts around CBC:
entry.content: Removed "SAP S/4HANA" tag as this topic is CBC relevant.

entry.title: Re: Earmarked funds not updating in Funds Management
entry.content:
Hi!!

you have verify and activate the option customizing 

Activate/Deactivate Funds Management

regards!

 

AHET

entry.title: How to use SAP Customer Checkout managers API endpoints
entry.content:
The SAP Customer Checkout application provides point-of-sale management and real-time sales and stock updates.

But making your own requests to SAP Customer Checkout managers API can be a challenging task at first. For an introduction how to use SAP Customer Checkout managers API and how to make your own requests you can follow this guide.

Bruno API Client
Bruno is an Opensource API client that stores API requests in collections locally on your filesystem.

This guide uses Bruno as an API client, this is one of many possible clients to make it easier sending and troubleshooting API requests and saving them for future use. There will also be some advise that makes it easier to work with the SAP Customer Checkout manager and how to do that in Bruno. These tricks are possible on other API clients but will not be explained here.

Installation
If you want to use Bruno you can install it via the official Website or the Github page .

Usage
To show how to use API clients with SAP Customer Checkout manager lets go through this guide

Bruno setup
1. Install and open Bruno.

2. Click on Create Collection and fill out Name and Location.

3. Hover over the three dots and create a New Request.
 
4. To test if everything is working correctly, make a request with just the address of the manager (e.g. localhost:8080/ccos).

5. Click on the right arrow in the upper right corner to send this request. If the response contains 200 OK you are good to continue, otherwise check if you entered the correct address and that the manager is currently running.


Environment

In the environment you can set variables with complicated content or changing values and give them fitting names. This will be a huge help when it comes to csrf tokens later in this guide.

Let us start with creating the Environment.

1. In the upper right corner click on No Environment then Configure.

2. Create a new Environment and give it a name.

3. To add new Variables click on Add Variable and fill out Name and Value. As an example you can put the URL that you used in the setup.

4. Variables can be used with two braces around the name of the variable.


 
Setting csrf token automatically

Most of the POST/PUT request need an attached CSRF token. Theses tokens expire after some time, therefore it is a good idea to use environment variables to set and replace them automatically.

1. Create new Post request to the endpoint "/api/auth/admin"


2. Change the body type to JSON and add login credentials as seen in the image. Enter your own values at "USERNAME" and "PASSWORD".


3. To set csrf tokens automatically add the following script under Post Response in the Script tab:
 
let data = res.getBody();
if(data && data.session) {
    bru.setEnvVar("csrfToken", data.session.csrfToken);
}
 


4. Every time this request is executed, the csrf token variable is replaced with the new value. If the csrf token runs out now you can just execute the login command again and it is set to a valid value again.

Post example

If you want to add new values to the manager you can use POST/PUT requests to do so. When using those requests you have to add your new values and the csrf token.

1. Look for the endpoint to create the desired input. In this case it is the endpoint to add coupons.


2. Go to the body and insert the necessary data in the JSON Format


3. To authorize the request add the csrf token with "x-csrf-token" as the name. Set the csrf token as described in "Setting csrf token automatically"

API Documentation
A list commonly used API Endpoints can be found in swagger at "/ccos/api-documentation".
If this page is diasabled in your installation you can enable it in the CCOm configuration General -> API Settings -> Enable API documentation page

entry.title: Re: Which one is preferred: Adapt UI or Adaptation Projects?
entry.content: Yes, key user application variants (as well as deployed developer adaptation projects) can be assigned to users similar to how custom apps are: https://help.sap.com/docs/ABAP_PLATFORM_NEW/a7b390faab1140c087b8926571e942b7/877a1b392b7f466e8be3b338ea14a099.html?version=202009.latest&locale=en-US Administrator needs to create a target mapping and a static app launcher.

entry.title: Re: Earmarked funds not updating in Funds Management
entry.content: Hi, yes. with and without AA derivation.

entry.title: HCM record several data records with one table
entry.content:
Hi you all,

I have a question about creating several qualifications via a table, for example.
So far, we have used the SAP-PA background service for this. We have created a process via HRASR_DT and also a form scenario. If you only want to enter one entry, it works, but if you want to enter more than one entry in the table, the second entry is overwritten and only one entry is posted in the background.


So if Quali 1 fields are in SAP_PA instance 1 and Quali 2 fields are in SAP_PA 2 instance, then it works - if both are in one then not, even when using different "Data record index" for these fields 

Do you have any Idea how i could fix that ? 

Thanks 
Dominik

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Flexibility: Adapting UIs for Specific User Roles
entry.content:
@RaminS - the feature described above in this blogpost is only available for BTP ABAP environment, but we are planning to enable this feature for all platforms.

We do offer alternatives for S/4 on-premise like key user application variants.

The difference between the two features is: key user app variants create a new application with its own app id, meaning you also need to create new tile/target mapping to access this in on-prem. The feature above does not create a new application, all adaptations are part of the same original application, so no new target mapping needed. The advantage of the new feature is that it enables smooth app2app navigation, which can be tricky with app variants as you have to reconfigure all navigation to follow the new id.

Hope that helps!

entry.title: SBPA - Stopping an automation for a day
entry.content:
Hi Team,
Please help me clarify some of my doubts.
1)How can i stop an automation for a day and after a day continue with the next steps.Is there any way to do it.
My requirement is , once i have completed some steps , i should wait for sometime(a day or so) to execute the next steps.How can i achieve this.
2)If i have to wait for hours between the steps, what is the best approach to implement.
3)Is it better to loop in the screen and check the condition , if yes how much time should i do it(what is the best practice)
Regards,
Keerthana

entry.title: Re: SAP Datasphere: OData Connector for PowerBi
entry.content:
hi @olaf_fischer 

Is there any update or solution for odata live connection for PowerBI?

entry.title: Re: SAPUI5 Flexibility: Adapting UIs for Specific User Roles
entry.content:
@Frank1 - sorry for the long delay! 

1. No, the scenarios in the blog post are only supported for BTP ABAP environment. This means it is currently not supported for S/4 private cloud, on-premise, public cloud. But we are working on enabling this!

2. We typically recommend to do the adaptation in the development or q-system first and then transport them to the productive system. Since the changes will potentially affect many end-users of your company it is helpful to have a quality stage to verify that your changes are in fact needed and do not cause any side effects.

entry.title: source file does not exist 3721 error
entry.content:
0
No one is currently editing
 

Sap attachments not accessible

entry.title: Re: SAP B1 for HANA Email Alerts showing a common Subject
entry.content: its not an empty subject, i have mentioned the subject i need in the both Alerts in SAP and Analytical Portal, but when the email is sent the subject is changed to the standard message.

entry.title: Re: Comments on BW/BPC live Connections in SAP Analytics Cloud
entry.content:
I must add in SAC you have to put Read, in the Comments section of the roles you created. Otherwise users don't see the small green triangles in the corners of the commented cells. They can still see the comments though, if they click on the cell.

Many thanks for the good explanation.

Is there any new development of this feature? Users are not very happy that you cannot edit comments, or delete only your own comments...

Cheers!

entry.title: Re: Serving Media Data: Content Type application/json det...
entry.content: @gregorw  We added support of streaming with 'application/json' as content-type. The data field should be modelled as cds.LargeBinary. The feature to be provided with the next release of @sap/cds.

entry.title: FK08 Report
entry.content:
Hello SAP experts,

Is there any way to track the FK08 approvals by change ?

By using the tables CDHDR and CDPOS it is not possible to link the FK02 change to its FK08 approval.

Is there any way to track that?

Thanks & Regards

entry.title: Issue with Decimal Conversion After Upgrading @sap/cds from 7.9.3 to 8.0.3
entry.content:
I've recently upgraded @Sap/cds from version 7.9.3 to 8.0.3 and encountered an issue with Decimal conversion. Specifically, I'm getting the following error:

"Value is not a valid Decimal"

I referred to the CAP release notes for June 2024 and followed the instructions to enable the new ieee754compatible feature as mentioned 

https://cap.cloud.sap/docs/releases/jun24#new-option-cds-features-ieee754compatible 

Here's what I added to my package.json file:

"cds": {
"features": {
"ieee754compatible": true
}
}

Despite this, I'm still facing the same issue. Has anyone else experienced this problem or have any suggestions on how to resolve it?

Thanks in advance for your help!

Hi David,

Thanks for the Reply! As suggested I did put Console parameters in my code and captured Developer Tools logs. Please see the screenshot.

 

 

Regards,

Ramesh

entry.title: Hello, while creating the project in PS, the wrong project type was selected and the YOY card is not
Re: SAP Fiori for SAP S/4HANA - Empowering Your Homepage: Enabling My Home for SAP S/4HANA 2023 FPS0
entry.content:
@Setu_Saxena Is there a Admin guide to set up the My Home with the steps from this blog?

the official product documentation for S4H 2023 FPS 01 still refers to the Fiori Launchpad, without any reference to the My Home page.

SAP Fiori Launchpad Features | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: GST Relevance for Free of Charge Deliveries in India
entry.content:
Dear Community,

 

we have an implementation project in India and there is the legal requirement to pay tax also on sales orders without charge.

The customer shall not get any information about the tax and there should also no open item on FI side.

The means we want to calculate the tax before we are deducting the 100% discount (or on basis of the original net amount) and want to post it with the billing document on the tax account and on an accruals account, since we do not want to have it on the receivables account. 

We found information for the for on Prem systems and tried to rebuild it in the public cloud but without success.

Independent what we are doing in customizing we are getting accounting errors (without specific information in the error) or it is not a real tax posting.

Do you know how to set up the functionality within the public cloud? Is there any process you know how to solve the requirement?

Thank you in advance!

Kind regards,

entry.title: Re: Optimized Story Experience - SAP SuccessFactors People Analytics
entry.content:
Dear SAP experts,

 

I would also like to know if you could share the timeline/deadline of the recommended/needed change from Classical to Optimized version.

Thanks a lot and BR,

Araceli

entry.title: Re: Modeling a basic Hierarchy with Directory in SAP Datasphere
entry.content:
@Sefan_Linders, thank you for that, correcting the mapping in the HwD Node Type Values appears to be most of the issue resolved. The hierarchy is still showing the Node ID for the Key which I find a bit odd. I suppose if I cannot find a way to resolve this I can substitute the numeric IDs with the corresponding actual dimension IDs.

I have not yet fully followed your example verbatim because I am hoping to try and avoid using Text tables (the client is a single language business and it thus seems unnecessary). Given that I still have a deficiency I will try using the text tables and also try using views over the base tables as Roberth has mentioned above.

Something that I have found worrying during my many attempts to get this to work is that the configuration only appears to have been updated when I delete the Fact view and Analytical model and re-build them from scratch. For example I now have a mostly working model, as shown above, where the fact data is associated with the REP_ITEM dimension. I have other models that are also associated with the REP_ITEM dimension where the hierarchy for the exact same dimension is not being displayed correctly. Have you experience this, could this be the result of some form of caching?
